description_long: This object is a detailed scale model of a traditional East Asian pagoda, featuring five distinct stories with wide, overhanging roofs that decrease in size as they ascend. Each roof tier is adorned with gold-colored trim and has slightly upturned eaves, a hallmark of classic pagoda architecture. The structure is mounted on a polished, dark wooden base, which provides stability and a sense of formality. At the apex of the pagoda sits an ornate finial, resembling a spire or stupa, which is typical in such designs. The lower level includes small red accents, possibly representing doors or windows, adding a touch of color contrast. The overall appearance is elegant and symmetrical, with careful attention to the layered roof structure and decorative details.
description: A five-story East Asian pagoda model with upturned eaves, gold trim, and an ornate finial, set on a polished wooden base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance area with red accents and the full layered roof structure.
description_view_1: Rear view with the same tiered roofs but without visible entrance details, emphasizing the symmetry.
description_view_2: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof eaves and the central finial at the top.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 2, showing the stacked roofs and decorative spire.
